source_pr.title: planner: refactor Join and Limit's ResolveIndices
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #45758 , close #45805 

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

In https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/45158, we use `ResolveIndices` for the inline projection's column matching.

It's possible that the join/limit's child(ren)'s output columns are `[col0, col0, col1]`.
And if we call ResolveIndices, the two `col0` from join/limit will resolve their indexes to the first col0. But actually, the first col0 should resolve to the first col0 of its child(ren). The second should resolve to the second of its child(ren).

This pr aims to solve this issue.

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes a planner ResolveIndices correctness issue around duplicate columns in Join/Limit outputs. The related Bazel test suite is flagged `flaky=True`, but this catalog entry does not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or explain why the behavior is intermittent. To avoid guessing a concurrency category from the Bazel `race` flag, classify as insufficient evidence.
fix_pattern: Fix ResolveIndices behavior for Join/Limit when child outputs contain duplicate columns; flaky quarantine signal exists but no concrete flaky signature captured here.
